q.en: When and where was Felix Baumgartner born?
q.zh: 费利克斯·鲍姆加特纳出生在何时何地？

options.en: In 1969 in Salzburg, Austria
options.zh: 1969年，奥地利萨尔茨堡

options.en: In 1960 in Vienna, Austria
options.zh: 1960年，奥地利维也纳

options.en: In 1975 in Salzburg, Austria
options.zh: 1975年，奥地利萨尔茨堡

options.en: In 1969 in Munich, Germany
options.zh: 1969年，德国慕尼黑

explain.en: The book states Felix was born in 1969 in Salzburg, Austria, and loved being high in the air from a young age.
explain.zh: 书中写道，费利克斯于1969年出生在奥地利萨尔茨堡，从小就很喜欢待在高空。

q.en: In 1999, Felix set his first world record by jumping from which building?
q.zh: 1999年，费利克斯从哪座建筑跳下来，创造了他的第一个世界纪录？

options.en: The Petronas Towers in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
options.zh: 马来西亚吉隆坡的双子塔

options.en: The Taipei 101 building in Taiwan
options.zh: 台湾的台北101大楼

options.en: The Empire State Building in New York
options.zh: 纽约的帝国大厦

options.en: The Bridge of the Americas in Panama
options.zh: 巴拿马的美洲大桥

explain.en: He sneaked in disguised as a businessman and jumped 1,479 feet from the Petronas Towers, then the world's tallest buildings.
explain.zh: 他伪装成商人溜进去，从当时世界最高的双子塔跳下，高度为1,479英尺。

q.en: In the glossary, the word 'claustrophobia' (page 10) means what?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“claustrophobia”（第10页）一词是什么意思？

options.en: The intense fear of closed or small spaces
options.zh: 对封闭或小空间的强烈恐惧

options.en: The love of jumping from high places
options.zh: 对从高处跳下的喜爱

options.en: A device that senses changes in conditions
options.zh: 一种感知环境变化的装置

options.en: The ability to keep working toward a hard goal
options.zh: 坚持努力实现艰难目标的能力

explain.en: The glossary defines claustrophobia as 'the intense fear of closed or small spaces,' which Felix developed inside his suit.
explain.zh: 词汇表将 claustrophobia 定义为“对封闭或小空间的强烈恐惧”，费利克斯就是在防护服里患上了这种恐惧症。

q.en: Why did Felix need a special pressurized suit for his big jump?
q.zh: 为什么费利克斯的大跳需要特制的加压防护服？

options.en: At over 120,000 feet there is almost no air pressure, which could be deadly
options.zh: 在12万英尺以上几乎没有气压，可能致命

options.en: To keep him cool while swimming in the ocean
options.zh: 为了让他游泳时保持凉爽

options.en: Because the suit made him fall faster than sound
options.zh: 因为防护服让他下落得比音速还快

options.en: To hide his identity from security guards
options.zh: 为了向保安隐藏他的身份

explain.en: He planned to jump from over 120,000 feet—more than four times Mount Everest's height—where there is almost no air pressure, so without protection his body's liquid would turn to gas.
explain.zh: 他计划从12万多英尺（超过四倍珠峰高度）处跳下，那里几乎没有气压，若没有防护，体内的液体会变成气体而要了命。

q.en: The book says Felix jumped from over 120,000 feet. How does that compare to Mount Everest?
q.zh: 书上说费利克斯从12万多英尺的高空跳下。这与珠穆朗玛峰相比如何？

options.en: It is more than four times the height of Mount Everest
options.zh: 是珠穆朗玛峰高度的四倍多

options.en: It is about the same height as Mount Everest
options.zh: 与珠穆朗玛峰高度差不多

options.en: It is less than half the height of Mount Everest
options.zh: 还不到珠穆朗玛峰高度的一半

options.en: It is exactly twice the height of Mount Everest
options.zh: 正好是珠穆朗玛峰高度的两倍

explain.en: Everest is 29,029 feet high; Felix's jump altitude of over 120,000 feet is more than four times higher.
explain.zh: 珠峰高29,029英尺，而费利克斯超过12万英尺的起跳高度是其四倍多。

q.en: Who was Felix's friend and mentor whose 1960 high-altitude record he aimed to break?
q.zh: 谁是费利克斯的朋友兼导师，其1960年的高空纪录正是他想要打破的？

options.en: Joe Kittinger
options.zh: 乔·基廷格

options.en: Alan Eustace
options.zh: 艾伦·尤斯塔斯

options.en: Robert Davidson
options.zh: 罗伯特·戴维森

options.en: Charles Kettering
options.zh: 查尔斯·凯特林

explain.en: Joe Kittinger set free-fall records in 1960 for the U.S. Air Force's Project Excelsior; Felix wanted to go higher and faster than him.
explain.zh: 乔·基廷格在1960年为美国空军“卓越计划”创造了自由落体纪录；费利克斯想比他飞得更高更快。

q.en: On his big jump, Felix reached a speed of 834 mph. Why is that speed especially notable?
q.zh: 在大跳中，费利克斯达到了每小时834英里的速度。为什么这个速度格外引人注目？

options.en: It was faster than the speed of sound
options.zh: 它比音速还快

options.en: It was the speed limit for airplanes
options.zh: 那是飞机的限速

options.en: It was slower than a cheetah runs
options.zh: 它比猎豹奔跑还慢

options.en: It matched the speed of a tsunami
options.zh: 它与海啸的速度相同

explain.en: The book notes 834 mph was more than 70 mph faster than the speed of sound—faster than anyone had ever traveled outside a vehicle.
explain.zh: 书中提到每小时834英里比音速还快70多英里，是人类在无载具情况下达到过的最快速度。

q.en: At the start of the jump, the author writes that within four seconds Felix 'is little more than a white dot in the distance.' This description helps the reader understand...
q.zh: 起跳之初，作者写道仅仅四秒后费利克斯就“不过是远处一个小白点”。这种描写帮助读者理解……

options.en: How tiny and far away Felix became as he fell from the capsule
options.zh: 他从太空舱坠落时变得多么微小、多么遥远

options.en: That Felix was wearing a bright white costume on purpose
options.zh: 费利克斯是故意穿了一身亮白色的服装

options.en: That the camera could not follow him anymore
options.zh: 摄像机已经再也拍不到他了

options.en: That it was snowing high in the sky
options.zh: 高空正在下雪

explain.en: The image of a 'white dot' shows how quickly Felix shrank to a tiny speck against the huge expanse of Earth below.
explain.zh: “小白点”的意象说明费利克斯很快就在广袤的地球背景中缩成了一个小点。

q.en: The book says Felix 'doesn't think of himself as a thrill seeker.' Why does the author include this?
q.zh: 书上说费利克斯“并不认为自己是个寻求刺激的人”。作者为什么要写这一点？

options.en: To show he focuses on careful planning and teamwork, not just excitement
options.zh: 为了说明他注重周密计划与团队合作，而非单纯追求刺激

options.en: To prove he was afraid of heights
options.zh: 为了证明他有恐高症

options.en: To explain why he never broke any records
options.zh: 为了解释他为何从未打破纪录

options.en: To suggest he did not like his team
options.zh: 为了暗示他不喜欢自己的团队

explain.en: Felix says success comes from planning every jump and thinking through scenarios with his team so no one gets hurt.
explain.zh: 费利克斯说成功来自对每次跳跃的周密计划，以及与团队把各种情况都想清楚，确保无人受伤。

q.en: At the end, the author mentions Alan Eustace's 2014 jump from 135,890 feet. Why include this?
q.zh: 在结尾处，作者提到了艾伦·尤斯塔斯2014年从135,890英尺高度的跳跃。为什么要写这个？

options.en: To show that high-altitude records kept being broken after Felix's achievement
options.zh: 为了说明在费利克斯之后，高空跳纪录仍在不断被打破

options.en: To prove Felix's record can never be beaten
options.zh: 为了证明费利克斯的纪录永远无法被超越

options.en: To explain how Felix trained for his jump
options.zh: 为了说明费利克斯是如何训练的

options.en: To describe the balloon used in 1960
options.zh: 为了描述1960年使用的气球

explain.en: Eustace broke one of Felix's altitude records in 2014, showing the story of record-setting jumps continued beyond Felix.
explain.zh: 尤斯塔斯在2014年打破了费利克斯的一项高度纪录，说明破纪录的历程在费利克斯之后仍在延续。

prompt.en: Felix said, 'Sometimes you have to go up really high to understand how small you are.' What do you think he meant, and have you ever felt that way?
prompt.zh: 费利克斯说：“有时候你必须飞得很高，才能明白自己是多么渺小。”你认为他是什么意思？你有过类似的感觉吗？

en: Think about how seeing Earth from far away might change a person's view of their own problems.
Give an example from your own life when a new perspective made something feel smaller or less scary.
zh: 想想从很远的地方看地球，会如何改变一个人对自己烦恼的看法。
举一个你自己的例子，说明换一个视角如何让某事显得更小或没那么可怕。
